intro: Theaphylla laxa, commonly known as the Palaichilia fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Theaphylla laxa include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, Theaphylla laxa is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions to help alleviate respiratory symptoms, and is also used in cough syrups and expectorants to promote mucus clearance.
